user_message: Can you help me map the stakeholders? The customer (Coho Winery) is asking about executive brief.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-12 12:25 EST
Subject: executive brief - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about executive brief. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
Date: 2024-01-18 16:07 EST
Subject: Follow-up: executive brief Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with executive brief. What are the next steps from your side?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF16-C trap (Skips the 'waiting on whom' question); produces complete Thread summary, key commitments/asks identified, waiting-on-whom assessment, draft response matching
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF16-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF16-C failure mode (Skips the 'waiting on whom' question)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct executive brief decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF16-C -- Skips the 'waiting on whom' question
